$comment: Holo Privacy the agentprivacy / blades framework (https://github.com/mitchuski/agentprivacy-docs) natively encoded for Hologram OS, expressed entirely in W3C privacy vocabulary (DPV + ODRL + Verifiable Credentials) so it interoperates with existing internet apps and agents. The user is the W3C VC holder / DPV data subject; a delegate agent acts for them but only ever receives minimal, purpose-bound selective disclosures (W3C Verifiable Presentations, IETF SD-JWT pattern), enforced at the agent (MCP) seam. Hand-authored (like apps/terms & apps/liberty) NOT generated from the hub catalog, so tools/gen-app-defs.mjs never touches it. Built by tools/build-app.mjs.
name: Holo Privacy
summary: Control what an AI agent may learn about you minimum disclosure, for a stated purpose, never enough to track you.

description.p: An agent acting for you needs some data but should never become a surveillance channel.
Every disclosure is minimal, bound to a stated purpose, and issued as a signed, verifiable credential you are the data subject.

ul: You control what each agent sees
The agent-facing companion to Holo Terms

resources.name: Holo Privacy disclosure policies (UOR)
resources.description: The standard disclosure policies (W3C ODRL Sets binding a DPV Purpose to the personal-data categories it may touch): MINIMAL, MINIMAL-VERIFY, CONTACT, IDENTITY, PAYMENT. Each is a content-addressed UOR object carrying its plain-language statement + machine policy and re-derives to its did:holo (Law L5).

tools.name: disclose_for_purpose
tools.description: Produce a W3C Verifiable Presentation that discloses only the personal-data claims the user's standing policy authorizes for a given DPV purpose and recipient (selective disclosure). Returns null if nothing is authorized (default-deny). Args: purpose (dpv:…), recipient, claims:[{category,name}].

tools.name: verify_disclosure
tools.description: Verify a W3C Verifiable Presentation against its personal-data commitment: re-derive its content address (Law L5), verify the holder's data-integrity proof, and recompute every disclosed SD-JWT digest. Reports what was disclosed and how much (n of m). Args: presentation, commitment.
